title: Hot Rap Songs

infobox.value: "[Not Like Us](/wiki/Not_Like_Us "Not Like Us")" | Kendrick Lamar | [[19]](#cite_note-Not_Like_Us_Rap_chart-19)[[20]](#cite_note-Not_Like_Us_Hiphop_chart-20)

"[Old Town Road](/wiki/Old_Town_Road "Old Town Road")" | [Lil Nas X](/wiki/Lil_Nas_X "Lil Nas X") featuring [Billy Ray Cyrus](/wiki/Billy_Ray_Cyrus "Billy Ray Cyrus") | 2019 | [[21]](#cite_note-:0-21)
"[Industry Baby](/wiki/Industry_Baby "Industry Baby")" | Lil Nas X and [Jack Harlow](/wiki/Jack_Harlow "Jack Harlow") | 2021–22 | [[22]](#cite_note-22)

"[Hot Boyz](/wiki/Hot_Boyz_(song) "Hot Boyz (song)")" | [Missy "Misdemeanor" Elliott](/wiki/Missy_Elliott "Missy Elliott") featuring [Lil' Mo](/wiki/Lil%27_Mo "Lil' Mo"), [Nas](/wiki/Nas "Nas"), [Eve](/wiki/Eve_(rapper) "Eve (rapper)") and [Q-Tip](/wiki/Q-Tip_(musician) "Q-Tip (musician)") | 1999–2000 | [[23]](#cite_note-HotlineBling-23)
"[Best I Ever Had](/wiki/Best_I_Ever_Had "Best I Ever Had")" | Drake | 2009 | [[23]](#cite_note-HotlineBling-23)

(formerly known as **Hot Rap Tracks** and **Hot Rap Singles**) is a [chart](/wiki/Record_chart "Record chart") released weekly by *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_magazine "Billboard magazine")* in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). It lists the 25 most popular [hip-hop/rap](/wiki/Hip_hop_music "Hip hop music") songs, calculated weekly by airplay on rhythmic and [urban](/wiki/Urban_contemporary "Urban contemporary") radio stations and sales in hip hop-focused or exclusive markets. Streaming data, digital downloads and All-Format radio stations were added to the methodology of determining chart rankings in 2012.[[1]](#cite_note-1) From 1989 through 2001, it was based on how much the single sold in that given week.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American rap record chart published by *Billboard*

**Hot Rap Songs** (formerly known as **Hot Rap Tracks** and **Hot Rap Singles**) is a [chart](/wiki/Record_chart "Record chart") released weekly by *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_magazine "Billboard magazine")* in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). It lists the 25 most popular [hip-hop/rap](/wiki/Hip_hop_music "Hip hop music") songs, calculated weekly by airplay on rhythmic and [urban](/wiki/Urban_contemporary "Urban contemporary") radio stations and sales in hip hop-focused or exclusive markets. Streaming data, digital downloads and All-Format radio stations were added to the methodology of determining chart rankings in 2012.[[1]](#cite_note-1) From 1989 through 2001, it was based on how much the single sold in that given week.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Chart statistics and other facts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Chart statistics and other facts")]

### Artists with the most number-one singles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Artists with the most number-one singles")]

| Number | Artist | Source |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 31 | [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(musician) "Drake (musician)") | [[3]](#cite_note-:1-3) |
| 12 | [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne") | [[4]](#cite_note-4) |
| 11 | [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West") | [[5]](#cite_note-5) |
| 10 | [Puff Daddy](/wiki/Puff_Daddy "Puff Daddy") | [[6]](#cite_note-6) |
| [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj") | [[7]](#cite_note-7) |
| 8 | [LL Cool J](/wiki/LL_Cool_J "LL Cool J") | [[8]](#cite_note-8) |
| 7 | [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent") | [[9]](#cite_note-9) |
| [Cardi B](/wiki/Cardi_B "Cardi B") | [[10]](#cite_note-10) |
| [Kendrick Lamar](/wiki/Kendrick_Lamar "Kendrick Lamar") | [[11]](#cite_note-11) |
| [T.I.](/wiki/T.I. "T.I.") | [[12]](#cite_note-12) |
| [Travis Scott](/wiki/Travis_Scott "Travis Scott") | [[13]](#cite_note-13) |

### Artists with the most consecutive weeks at number one

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Artists with the most consecutive weeks at number one")]

* 29 weeks - [Macklemore](/wiki/Macklemore "Macklemore") and [Ryan Lewis](/wiki/Ryan_Lewis "Ryan Lewis") ("[Thrift Shop](/wiki/Thrift_Shop "Thrift Shop")", "[Can't Hold Us](/wiki/Can%27t_Hold_Us "Can't Hold Us")")
* 21 weeks – Lil Wayne ("[Lollipop](/wiki/Lollipop_(Lil_Wayne_song) "Lollipop (Lil Wayne song)")", "[A Milli](/wiki/A_Milli "A Milli")")
* 20 weeks - [T-Pain](/wiki/T-Pain "T-Pain") ("[Good Life](/wiki/Good_Life_(Kanye_West_song) "Good Life (Kanye West song)")", "[Low](/wiki/Low_(Flo_Rida_song) "Low (Flo Rida song)")"); T.I. ("[Whatever You Like](/wiki/Whatever_You_Like "Whatever You Like")", "[Live Your Life](/wiki/Live_Your_Life_(T.I._song) "Live Your Life (T.I. song)")"); Drake "[I'm on One](/wiki/I%27m_on_One "I'm on One")", "[Headlines](/wiki/Headlines_(Drake_song) "Headlines (Drake song)")", [Lil Nas X](/wiki/Lil_Nas_X "Lil Nas X") ("[Old Town Road](/wiki/Old_Town_Road "Old Town Road")")
* 19 weeks – [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent") ("[Candy Shop](/wiki/Candy_Shop "Candy Shop")", "[Hate It Or Love It](/wiki/Hate_It_Or_Love_It "Hate It Or Love It")", "[Just A Lil Bit](/wiki/Just_A_Lil_Bit "Just A Lil Bit")")

Note: Above chart only considers songs that charted in 2004 or later

### Artists simultaneously occupying the top three positions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Artists simultaneously occupying the top three positions")]

* [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent"): April 2, 2005[[14]](#cite_note-Billboard-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

1. "[Candy Shop](/wiki/Candy_Shop "Candy Shop")" (featuring [Olivia](/wiki/Olivia_(singer) "Olivia (singer)")) (No. 1 April 2, 2005)
2. "[Hate It or Love It](/wiki/Hate_It_or_Love_It "Hate It or Love It")" (with [The Game](/wiki/The_Game_(rapper) "The Game (rapper)")) (No. 2 April 2, 2005)
3. "[How We Do](/wiki/How_We_Do_(song) "How We Do (song)")" (with The Game) (No. 3 April 2, 2005)[[14]](#cite_note-Billboard-14)[[16]](#cite_note-October-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)
4. "[I'm On One](/wiki/I%27m_On_One "I'm On One")" (with [DJ Khaled](/wiki/DJ_Khaled "DJ Khaled"), [Rick Ross](/wiki/Rick_Ross "Rick Ross") & [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne")) (No. 1 October 8, No. 2 October 15, and No. 3 October 22, 2011)
5. "[Headlines](/wiki/Headlines_(Drake_song) "Headlines (Drake song)")" (No. 2 October 8 and No. 1 October 15, and October 22, 2011)
6. "[She Will](/wiki/She_Will "She Will")" (with Lil Wayne) (No. 3 October 8 and October 15, and No. 2 October 22, 2011)

### Songs with the most weeks at number one

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Songs with the most weeks at number one")]

| Weeks | Song | Artist | Year(s) | Source |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 47 | "[Luther](/wiki/Luther_(song) "Luther (song)")" | [Kendrick Lamar](/wiki/Kendrick_Lamar "Kendrick Lamar") and [SZA](/wiki/SZA "SZA") | 2024–25 | [[18]](#cite_note-18) |
| 26 | "[Not Like Us](/wiki/Not_Like_Us "Not Like Us")" | Kendrick Lamar | [[19]](#cite_note-Not_Like_Us_Rap_chart-19)[[20]](#cite_note-Not_Like_Us_Hiphop_chart-20) |
| 20 | "[Old Town Road](/wiki/Old_Town_Road "Old Town Road")" | [Lil Nas X](/wiki/Lil_Nas_X "Lil Nas X") featuring [Billy Ray Cyrus](/wiki/Billy_Ray_Cyrus "Billy Ray Cyrus") | 2019 | [[21]](#cite_note-:0-21) |
| 19 | "[Industry Baby](/wiki/Industry_Baby "Industry Baby")" | Lil Nas X and [Jack Harlow](/wiki/Jack_Harlow "Jack Harlow") | 2021–22 | [[22]](#cite_note-22) |
| 18 | "[Hot Boyz](/wiki/Hot_Boyz_(song) "Hot Boyz (song)")" | [Missy "Misdemeanor" Elliott](/wiki/Missy_Elliott "Missy Elliott") featuring [Lil' Mo](/wiki/Lil%27_Mo "Lil' Mo"), [Nas](/wiki/Nas "Nas"), [Eve](/wiki/Eve_(rapper) "Eve (rapper)") and [Q-Tip](/wiki/Q-Tip_(musician) "Q-Tip (musician)") | 1999–2000 | [[23]](#cite_note-HotlineBling-23) |
| "[Fancy](/wiki/Fancy_(Iggy_Azalea_song) "Fancy (Iggy Azalea song)")" | [Iggy Azalea](/wiki/Iggy_Azalea "Iggy Azalea") featuring [Charli XCX](/wiki/Charli_XCX "Charli XCX") | 2014 | [[23]](#cite_note-HotlineBling-23) |
| "[Hotline Bling](/wiki/Hotline_Bling "Hotline Bling")" | [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(musician) "Drake (musician)") | 2015–16 | [[23]](#cite_note-HotlineBling-23) |
| 17 | "[Panda](/wiki/Panda_(song) "Panda (song)")" | [Desiigner](/wiki/Desiigner "Desiigner") | 2016 | [[19]](#cite_note-Not_Like_Us_Rap_chart-19) |
| "[Mood](/wiki/Mood_(24kGoldn_song) "Mood (24kGoldn song)")" | [24kGoldn](/wiki/24kGoldn "24kGoldn") featuring [Iann Dior](/wiki/Iann_Dior "Iann Dior") | 2020–21 | [[24]](#cite_note-24) |
| 15 | "[Best I Ever Had](/wiki/Best_I_Ever_Had "Best I Ever Had")" | Drake | 2009 | [[23]](#cite_note-HotlineBling-23) |
| "[Thrift Shop](/wiki/Thrift_Shop "Thrift Shop")" | [Macklemore & Ryan Lewis](/wiki/Macklemore_%26_Ryan_Lewis "Macklemore & Ryan Lewis") featuring [Wanz](/wiki/Wanz "Wanz") | 2013 | [[23]](#cite_note-HotlineBling-23) |
| "[Timber](/wiki/Timber_(Pitbull_song) "Timber (Pitbull song)")" | [Pitbull](/wiki/Pitbull_(rapper) "Pitbull (rapper)") featuring [Kesha](/wiki/Kesha "Kesha") | 2014 | [[23]](#cite_note-HotlineBling-23) |
| "[See You Again](/wiki/See_You_Again "See You Again")" | [Wiz Khalifa](/wiki/Wiz_Khalifa "Wiz Khalifa") featuring [Charlie Puth](/wiki/Charlie_Puth "Charlie Puth") | 2015 | [[23]](#cite_note-HotlineBling-23) |
| "[Rockstar](/wiki/Rockstar_(Post_Malone_song) "Rockstar (Post Malone song)")" | [Post Malone](/wiki/Post_Malone "Post Malone") featuring [21 Savage](/wiki/21_Savage "21 Savage") | 2017 | [[25]](#cite_note-25) |
| "[Lovin on Me](/wiki/Lovin_on_Me "Lovin on Me")" | Jack Harlow | 2023–24 | [[26]](#cite_note-26) |

### Self-replacement at number one

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Self-replacement at number one")]

#### Lead artist

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Lead artist")]

* [Bow Wow](/wiki/Bow_Wow_(rapper) "Bow Wow (rapper)") — "[Let Me Hold You](/wiki/Let_Me_Hold_You "Let Me Hold You")" (Bow Wow feat. Omarion) (7 weeks) → "[Like You](/wiki/Like_You_(Bow_Wow_song) "Like You (Bow Wow song)")" (Bow Wow feat. Ciara) (4 weeks) (September 10, 2005)
* [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne") — "[Lollipop](/wiki/Lollipop_(Lil_Wayne_song) "Lollipop (Lil Wayne song)")" (Lil Wayne feat. Static Major) (18 weeks) → "[A Milli](/wiki/A_Milli "A Milli")" (7 weeks) (July 26, 2008)
* [T.I.](/wiki/T.I. "T.I.") — "[Whatever You Like](/wiki/Whatever_You_Like "Whatever You Like")" (10 weeks) → "[Live Your Life](/wiki/Live_Your_Life_(T.I._song) "Live Your Life (T.I. song)")" (T.I. feat. Rihanna) (10 weeks) (November 29, 2008)
* [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(entertainer) "Drake (entertainer)") — "[Make Me Proud](/wiki/Make_Me_Proud "Make Me Proud")" (Drake feat. Nicki Minaj) (1 week) → "[The Motto](/wiki/The_Motto_(Drake_song) "The Motto (Drake song)")" (Drake feat. Lil Wayne) (14 weeks) (February 18, 2012)
* [Macklemore](/wiki/Macklemore "Macklemore") & [Ryan Lewis](/wiki/Ryan_Lewis "Ryan Lewis") — "[Thrift Shop](/wiki/Thrift_Shop_(song) "Thrift Shop (song)")" (Macklemore & Ryan Lewis feat. Wanz) (15 weeks) → "[Can't Hold Us](/wiki/Can%27t_Hold_Us "Can't Hold Us")" (Macklemore & Ryan Lewis feat. Ray Dalton) (14 weeks) (May 4, 2013)
* Drake — "[God's Plan](/wiki/God%27s_Plan_(song) "God's Plan (song)")" (11 weeks) → "[Nice For What](/wiki/Nice_for_What "Nice for What")" (8 weeks) (April 21, 2018)
* Drake — "Nice For What" (8 weeks) → "[In My Feelings](/wiki/In_My_Feelings "In My Feelings")" (11 weeks) (July 21, 2018)
* [Post Malone](/wiki/Post_Malone "Post Malone") — "[Sunflower (Spider-Man: Into The Spider-Verse)](/wiki/Sunflower_(Post_Malone_and_Swae_Lee_song) "Sunflower (Post Malone and Swae Lee song)")" (Post Malone & Swae Lee) (11 weeks) → "[Wow.](/wiki/Wow_(Post_Malone_song) "Wow (Post Malone song)")" (1 week) (April 6, 2019)

#### Featured artist

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Featured artist")]

* [T-Pain](/wiki/T-Pain "T-Pain") — "[Good Life](/wiki/Good_Life_(Kanye_West_song) "Good Life (Kanye West song)")" (Kanye West feat. T-Pain) (9 weeks) (November 3, 2007) → "[Low](/wiki/Low_(Flo_Rida_song) "Low (Flo Rida song)")" (Flo Rida feat. T-Pain) (11 weeks) (January 5, 2008)
* [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West") — "[Run This Town](/wiki/Run_This_Town "Run This Town")" (Jay-Z feat. Rihanna & Kanye West) (7 weeks) → "[Forever](/wiki/Forever_(Drake_song) "Forever (Drake song)")" (Drake feat. Kanye West, Lil Wayne, & Eminem) (1 week) (November 14, 2009)

#### Combined (lead and featured artist)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Combined (lead and featured artist)")]

* [50 Cent](/wiki/50_Cent "50 Cent") — "[Candy Shop](/wiki/Candy_Shop "Candy Shop")" (50 Cent feat. Olivia) (6 weeks) → "[Hate It or Love It](/wiki/Hate_It_or_Love_It "Hate It or Love It")" (The Game feat. 50 Cent) (4 weeks) (April 23, 2005) → "[Just a Lil Bit](/wiki/Just_a_Lil_Bit "Just a Lil Bit")" (50 Cent) (9 weeks) (May 21, 2005)
* [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(entertainer) "Drake (entertainer)") — "[Fancy](/wiki/Fancy_(Drake_song) "Fancy (Drake song)")" (Drake feat. T.I. & Swizz Beatz) (1 week) → "[Right Above It](/wiki/Right_Above_It "Right Above It")" (Lil Wayne feat. Drake) (5 weeks) (November 6, 2010)
* [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown_(American_entertainer) "Chris Brown (American entertainer)") — "[Look at Me Now](/wiki/Look_at_Me_Now_(Chris_Brown_song) "Look at Me Now (Chris Brown song)")" (Chris Brown feat. Lil Wayne & Busta Rhymes) (10 weeks) → "[My Last](/wiki/My_Last "My Last")" (Big Sean feat. Chris Brown) (2 weeks) (July 2, 2011)
* [2 Chainz](/wiki/2_Chainz "2 Chainz") — "[Mercy](/wiki/Mercy_(GOOD_Music_song) "Mercy (GOOD Music song)")" (Kanye West feat. Big Sean, Pusha T & 2 Chainz) (9 weeks) → "[No Lie](/wiki/No_Lie_(2_Chainz_song) "No Lie (2 Chainz song)")" (2 Chainz feat. Drake) (6 weeks) (September 8, 2012)
* [Travis Scott](/wiki/Travis_Scott "Travis Scott") — "[Zeze](/wiki/Zeze_(song) "Zeze (song)")" (Kodak Black feat. Travis Scott & Offset) (1 week) → "[SICKO MODE](/wiki/Sicko_Mode "Sicko Mode")" (Travis Scott) (10 weeks) (November 3, 2018)

### Total weeks at number one per decade

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Total weeks at number one per decade")]

#### 2000s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: 2000s")]

Total number weeks at number one as a lead or featured artist

1. Missy Elliott – 56 weeks
2. T.I – 49 weeks
3. Bow Wow – 40 weeks
4. Kanye West – 32 weeks
5. T-Pain – 29 weeks
6. Ludacris – 29 weeks
7. Lil Wayne – 28 weeks
8. Nelly – 25 weeks
9. Snoop Dogg – 20 weeks

#### 2010s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Rap_Songs&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: 2010s")]

Total number weeks at number one as a lead or featured artist

1. Drake – 125 weeks
2. Lil Wayne – 53 weeks
3. Macklemore & Ryan Lewis – 29 weeks
4. Post Malone – 28 weeks
5. Jay-Z – 25 weeks
6. Nicki Minaj – 25 weeks
7. Iggy Azalea – 24 weeks
8. Pitbull – 21 weeks
9. Rihanna – 20 weeks
10. Kanye West, Lil Nas X – 19 weeks
11. Eminem, Charli XCX – 18 weeks
